title: centaurea seridis var. sonchifolia
intro: Centaurea seridis var. sonchifolia, also known as Sow Thistle or Yellow Sow Thistle, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Centaurea seridis var. sonchifolia include treating urinary tract infections, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues such as constipation and bloating. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea or by drying the plant material to make a tincture or powder.
